text: Yeah.

text: Okay.

text: I'm pulling my hair out.

text: Do you feel like you start from zero and does it add any additional level of stress to you at all or anything?

text: Yeah.

text: I mean...

speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: That's great.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And I think our conversion rate is like 4% or 5%.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Okay, so kind of normal.

speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: We just don't get enough from new users to do that.

text: So one of the things I forgot to ask early on, which I think is probably stewing in the minds of some of the listeners is when you were starting out and you didn't have any income coming in, you weren't sure if this is going to work.
speaker: SPEAKER_03

speaker: SPEAKER_03

speaker: SPEAKER_01

speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: Let's say I'm a brand new developer.
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: I've created an app.
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: I've shipped it on the Play Store.
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: I just took screenshots and I put them up there.
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: How do I optimize it to kind of get the best results based upon your experience?
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: Well, first I'll say we're definitely not experts on Play Store listing optimization, but I can just tell you our experience.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Yeah, for sure.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And we've kind of taken the approach of imagine a new user is visiting your listing that's never seen it before.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: You got to tell them what your app or watch face does.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Tell them the story of your app.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Yeah.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And you want to convince them that this is something that will enhance their life in some way or solve some problem that they have.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And give them some sense that this is not a gimmicky app, that this is something that some real thought has gone into.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Yeah.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And of course, all the standard things like, you know, list things that, you know, if you have compatibility constraints, make sure you list those.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Or you have people complaining that it's not compatible with whatever weird device they have.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And, you know, I mean, Google Play has a whole list of guidelines that you have to follow.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And I wouldn't discount the price of art.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Obviously, when we first put stuff out, we just did exactly what you said.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: We put a screenshot in with some text above it, and that's how we started.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But once things started getting traction, we realized we need to do a little bit better and step it up.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And that's when we started contracting with graphic artists to help us just upgrade the look and feel and make it look a lot more professional.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Is that the app itself or the listing or both?
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: The app itself and the listing and pretty much every graphic and UX.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: The app icons.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Yeah.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Yeah.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: The marketing, any marketing graphics that we do have.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I think that circles back to like, you can't do everything.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Yes.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: As a developer, right?
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: I mean, you kind of have to in the beginning.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Yeah.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: But you just can't be good at everything.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And you have to really think about...
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: okay, I want to focus my time on the things that I'm really good at and I can really produce and then spend a little bit of money and let some people that are really good at those other things do those things.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And for us, we recognize that we are absolutely atrocious at creating graphics.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And so we completely outsource that.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Yeah.
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: Okay.
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: Would you say, I mean, I 100% agree with that.
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: Just focus on your strengths and then outsource everything else.
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: That's exactly what I do too.
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: When did you decide that, hey, we don't have an explainer video, which is the, like you said, the YouTube video that goes in your playlisting.
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: And when did you decide to do that?
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: And then what made you decide, or did you at first make your own and then decide to go hire someone?
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: Or was it just, hey, we just know we can't do this.
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: We're going to go hire somebody.
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: I think I opened Premiere Pro.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: That's as far as I got.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: That's just crazy.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: What?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Yeah.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Remember we talked about the influencer?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And so we actually used that video for a long time.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And it was challenging because once it's on YouTube and somebody...
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: produces it, then you start adding more options and features, and the graphics start morphing, and then they're not exactly what's in the video.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So at some point, that kind of breaks, and you're like, we need to do something new.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Yeah, but we used the YouTube influencers video in our Play Store listing.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Yeah, for quite a while.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Yeah, and we asked him if that was okay.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And it drove a lot of traffic to his YouTube site, so he was pretty happy, I think.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Yeah.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: But then, you know, once we had more revenue and we decided to hire someone to create a better video.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: That's cool.
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: And then when you did that, did you give them... How did you do that?
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: Did you get them on a call and walk them through, hey, here's our app, here's what it does.
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: We want to showcase these features.
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: And then did you just kind of let them do their magic and then refine from there?
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: Yeah.
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: Yeah, I mean, it's kind of a long story for us, but just the short version, the short answer is yes.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And I think the important step is that the videographer is storyboarded.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And then we iterated back and forth on the storyboard of like, what's the story we want to tell?
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And once we got that settled, then we went into production.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And we actually filmed with people.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Oh, wow, that's cool.
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: And it's on our Play Store listing.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: But there's also lots of like people that do animated explainer videos.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And I think those would be fine too.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Okay.
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: Interesting.
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: So where do you see squeaky dog studios going from now?
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: Is it just more support building more features for the photo watch face, more watch faces or what, what's next?
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: Yeah, I think let me back up and answer another question, which is kind of how do we spend our time?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Because I think that's been a big challenge is how do we figure out, you know, among these four apps, what?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And then we had more that we ended up pulling.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Where do we spend our time?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Because we only have so much of it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And because we don't have another developer, we can't think about hiring a team until we get the subscription model.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And things need updating because Google requires it or they're going to pull your app.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: We do that.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But let's say we have another feature idea.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: We want to add or change something.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Do we go back and change that?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Or do we continue marching on this app that's actually the most popular?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Yeah, I'm about to bring it up.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Yeah, there's been a big tension because we can spend a month adding a new feature to a different app that's got thousands of downloads versus a month adding a new feature to PhotoWare.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And maybe that actually has a broader value base.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But for the most part, to answer the question that you did ask, we've been actually working on a refresh of Photoware.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Completely new, rethought.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: The UI is completely different, and we're adding a lot more features.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: It's a much bigger scope.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: We're doing it all in Kotlin instead of Java.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So that's fun.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So sometimes we do have to go back to look at Java, and I'm like, ugh.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: All right.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: How did we ever program in this language?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Right, exactly.
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: So that's taken up a lot of our time.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: We thought, oh, we'd get this out December of 2019, I think, and then... We're still working on it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: We're still working on it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But our goal is now to get it out at the end of this year, and we've made a lot of progress, so I'm feeling pretty confident about it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Yeah, I'll just add that for this refresh, we've brought on a UX designer.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: We're using...
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: kind of a prototyping tool.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And it's a lot more complex.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: It's called Figma.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And we're going down a lot of permutations and we're uncovering a lot of things that normally we wouldn't have uncovered until we were deep in development.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And so...
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: It's been an experience and I'm looking forward to getting the refreshed app in the Play Store.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: But this is also one of those cases where we're like, going back to your first question is about, you know, when do you actually put this out?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Because we could go down this rabbit hole and we could spend years...
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: thinking and iterating on what should this do and how should it look.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And we're having to make some tough calls because we're like, okay, we need to just get this out.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And let's just simplify this and not have that at first and not have this in first.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Yeah.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Well, we have some more ability to drag this out because we have an app in the Play Store that is making revenue.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And this is kind of a refresh of it.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: So we're able to spend some time on it, which we didn't have that luxury in the beginning.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Do you find it difficult to keep certain features that you maybe want to just can in general, but know other people use?
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: Or are you just planning on keeping everything and just kind of refreshing it?
speaker: SPEAKER_03

text: We have looked at analytics.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I think that's really important.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: We haven't talked about that.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But having analytics across the apps help you understand what are features that people are using and talking about monetization, then that can help inform your monetization scheme as well.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So sometimes we do just make a call and say, this isn't a feature that people are using.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So we don't really need to focus on that or minimize it in a certain way.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And then we know these are features that people are using and they really like, like font choices.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So let's add more and maybe even add a font pack that they have to purchase.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So I think that having those analytics really help.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And we combine that with the feedback we get directly from customers.

text: Right.

text: That's cool.

text: Yeah.
